evals.prompt: test accessibility for my Button component at src/components/Button/Button.tsx — it has variants (primary, secondary, danger), can be disabled, and renders an icon next to the label
evals.expected_output: A .a11y.feature file with scenarios covering a baseline axe audit, keyboard operability, accessible name, and color contrast, plus a .a11y.steps.tsx file implementing those scenarios with vitest-axe and RTL. Tests should pass.
assertions: A file src/components/Button/Button.a11y.feature is created
A file src/components/Button/Button.a11y.steps.tsx is created
The .a11y.feature file includes @a11y and @wcag-aa tags at the Feature level
The .a11y.feature file contains a baseline scenario: 'No automated accessibility violations are detected'
The .a11y.feature file uses parameterized steps with quoted strings for variants
The .a11y.feature file does NOT contain ARIA attribute names, axe-core rule IDs, or CSS selectors
The .a11y.steps.tsx file imports axe and toHaveNoViolations from vitest-axe
The .a11y.steps.tsx file imports @testing-library/jest-dom/vitest for semantic matchers
The .a11y.steps.tsx file calls expect.extend({ toHaveNoViolations }) at the top level
The .a11y.steps.tsx file passes render().container to axe(), not a screen query
The .a11y.steps.tsx file uses describeFeature and Scenario from @amiceli/vitest-cucumber
The .a11y.steps.tsx file uses AfterEachScenario with cleanup(), not beforeEach
All parameterized step callbacks use _ctx as first argument
Step matchers use {string} or {int} templates, not regex
The .a11y.steps.tsx file does NOT use .toBeTruthy() or .toBeDefined() for non-axe element assertions — uses toBeInTheDocument(), toHaveAccessibleName(), or toHaveFocus() instead
The scenario-to-requirement mapping is presented to the user before writing files
npm test passes for the Button component

evals.prompt: I need WCAG accessibility tests for the LoginForm component at src/components/LoginForm/LoginForm.tsx — it has email and password inputs, a submit button, and shows inline error messages on validation failure. I care about keyboard navigation, proper label association, and error identification for screen readers
evals.expected_output: A .a11y.feature file with scenarios for axe baseline, keyboard tab order through form fields, label-input association, and error message accessibility. A .a11y.steps.tsx with RTL and vitest-axe implementations.
assertions: A file src/components/LoginForm/LoginForm.a11y.feature is created
A file src/components/LoginForm/LoginForm.a11y.steps.tsx is created
The .a11y.feature includes a baseline axe audit scenario
The .a11y.feature includes a keyboard navigation scenario
The .a11y.feature includes a scenario about form labels being associated with inputs
The .a11y.feature includes a scenario about error messages being accessible
The .a11y.feature describes error identification in user-facing language, not aria-describedby or role=alert jargon
The .a11y.steps.tsx imports @testing-library/jest-dom/vitest
The .a11y.steps.tsx uses userEvent.tab() for keyboard navigation testing
The .a11y.steps.tsx uses expect(element).toHaveFocus() for focus assertions, not .toBeTruthy()
The .a11y.steps.tsx uses toBeInTheDocument() for element presence, not .toBeTruthy()
npm test passes for the LoginForm component

evals.prompt: I already have a11y tests for my Modal component but I need to add a color contrast scenario and a focus trap scenario. The existing files are at src/components/Modal/Modal.a11y.feature and src/components/Modal/Modal.a11y.steps.tsx
evals.expected_output: The existing .a11y.feature and .a11y.steps.tsx files are updated with new scenarios appended, without overwriting existing scenarios. Tests pass.
assertions: The skill reads the existing Modal.a11y.feature before modifying it
The skill reads the existing Modal.a11y.steps.tsx before modifying it
Previously existing scenarios in Modal.a11y.feature are preserved unchanged
A new color contrast scenario is added to Modal.a11y.feature
A new focus trap scenario is added to Modal.a11y.feature
Corresponding step definitions are added to Modal.a11y.steps.tsx
The color contrast step uses axe with runOnly targeting color-contrast rules
The focus trap step uses userEvent.tab() and expect(element).toHaveFocus(), not .toBeTruthy()
The @a11y tag is still present at the Feature level
New assertions do NOT use .toBeTruthy() for element presence — use semantic matchers
npm test passes for the Modal component
